metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml` to the edit targets with the note that each new criterion ID needs a `- prompt: plan-review-<ID> / when: '${{ steps.assemble.outputs.include_<ID> }}'` entry in the finders batch `criteria:` block.
findings.finding: The `plan-review.yaml` workflow's finders batch step has a hardcoded `criteria:` list (plan-review.yaml:55-117); new criterion IDs require a matching `- prompt: plan-review-<ID> / when: '${{ steps.assemble.outputs.include_<ID> }}'` entry. The plan does not list `plan-review.yaml` as an edit target, so the new criteria will never be dispatched by the production workflow even if registered correctly.
findings.location: What (edit targets) section; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml:55-117

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `registry.py` to the edit-targets list with explicit callouts to update both `CANONICAL_LLM` and `AGENT_TIER` frozensets for the new criterion IDs.
findings.finding: The plan's edit targets list `criteria_routing.json` and new prompt files, but omits `registry.py` — which contains two hardcoded frozensets (`CANONICAL_LLM` at line 84 and `AGENT_TIER` at line 77) that must be updated for any new criterion ID. `validate_packaged_routing()` at `registry.py:426-438` enforces parity between `criteria_routing.json` keys and `CANONICAL_LLM`, emitting an error for any 'ORPHAN' entry (a key in criteria_routing.json not in CANONICAL_LLM). The AC proof `validate-routing exits 0` will fail unless `registry.py` is also edited.
findings.location: What (edit targets) section; registry.py:77-122, registry.py:426-438
findings.suggested_fix: Add to edit targets: `registry.py` — add new IDs to `CANONICAL_LLM` (line ~84) and `AGENT_TIER` (line ~77).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `registry.py` (to update `CANONICAL_LLM` and, if AGENT exec is chosen, `AGENT_TIER`) to the edit targets list.
findings.finding: The plan omits `registry.py` from its edit targets, but adding two new criterion IDs to `criteria_routing.json` requires also adding them to the `CANONICAL_LLM` frozenset (registry.py:84–122). The `validate_packaged_routing()` gate at registry.py:431–432 explicitly flags any routing entry not in `CANONICAL_LLM` as ORPHAN, causing CI failure. The plan's AC1 proof (`validate-routing` exits 0) would therefore fail unless `CANONICAL_LLM` is updated — but `registry.py` is not listed as an edit target.
findings.location: What (edit targets) section
findings.suggested_fix: Add `registry.py` to the 'What (edit targets)' section: add both new criterion IDs to `CANONICAL_LLM` (and to `AGENT_TIER` if they use `exec: AGENT`), matching the pattern used by T10/T11.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `registry.py` to the edit targets, explicitly noting that `CANONICAL_LLM` (and `AGENT_TIER` if the new criteria are AGENT-exec) must be updated alongside `criteria_routing.json` — otherwise `validate-routing` exits 1 with ORPHAN errors.
findings.finding: The edit targets list `criteria_routing.json` but omit `registry.py`. Adding new criterion IDs to `criteria_routing.json` without also adding them to the hardcoded `CANONICAL_LLM` frozenset in `registry.py` causes `validate_packaged_routing()` to report each new ID as an ORPHAN (`'routing entry {cid!r} is an ORPHAN (not in CANONICAL_LLM)'`), making `validate-routing` exit 1 — directly falsifying AC1's proof condition.
findings.location: What (edit targets) section; AC1 — 'Proof: `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing` exits 0'
findings.suggested_fix: In the edit targets, add: 'registry.py — add new criterion IDs to CANONICAL_LLM frozenset (and to AGENT_TIER if exec: AGENT), required for validate-routing to exit 0'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `registry.py` to the edit targets and specify that both new criterion IDs must be added to the `CANONICAL_LLM` frozenset (and, if exec:AGENT is desired but not in `exec` field of routing entry, to `AGENT_TIER`) before `validate-routing` can exit 0.
findings.finding: The plan omits `registry.py` from its edit targets, but `validate_packaged_routing()` (called by `validate-routing`) hard-rejects any `criteria_routing.json` entry whose ID is not in the hardcoded `CANONICAL_LLM` frozenset as an 'ORPHAN' (registry.py:431-432). Adding two new criteria to `criteria_routing.json` without also adding their IDs to `CANONICAL_LLM` will cause AC1's proof command to exit non-zero.
findings.location: What (edit targets) section; AC1 proof: `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing`
findings.suggested_fix: Add `registry.py` to the 'What (edit targets)' list: 'registry.py: add new criterion IDs to CANONICAL_LLM frozenset (required for validate_packaged_routing to pass the orphan check)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the concrete criterion IDs that will be registered (must be `Txx`-pattern for `is_overlay()` to recognize them as overlays, and must not collide with `G5`/`G6` etc. already in `CANONICAL_LLM`); update edit targets to include `registry.py` (`CANONICAL_LLM` + `AGENT_TIER` frozensets).
findings.finding: The plan names its two new overlay criteria 'G-5 prohibition-enumeration overlay' and 'G-10 CI-trigger/release-infra overlay' using gap-report labels, but never states the actual criterion IDs that would be registered in `criteria_routing.json`. Crucially, `G5` is already occupied in `CANONICAL_LLM` (the decomposition criterion, `plan_review_G5.md`). If the implementer uses `G5` or `G10` as IDs, `G5` collides with the existing decomposition criterion; if they use non-Txx IDs (e.g. `G-5`, `G-10`), `is_overlay()` at `registry.py:383` (`re.fullmatch(r'T\d+[a-e]?', crit_id)`) returns False, so `route_criteria`'s overlay skip-guard never fires — making these criteria always-run instead of overlay-gated.
findings.location: What (REDESIGNED) section — 'G-5 prohibition-enumeration overlay' / 'G-10 CI-trigger/release-infra overlay'; registry.py:383-384, orchestrator.py:364
findings.suggested_fix: Assign Txx IDs (e.g. T13 for prohibition-enumeration, T14 for CI-trigger), add both to `CANONICAL_LLM` and `AGENT_TIER` in `registry.py`, and add entries to `criteria_routing.json` with `overlay_routing: llm`. Add `registry.py` to the edit-targets list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the actual criterion IDs (e.g. T13/T14 to match `is_overlay()` regex, or explicitly acknowledge a `registry.py:is_overlay()` extension is needed) and confirm no collision with existing `G5`.
findings.finding: The plan uses the gap-report labels 'G-5' and 'G-10' as criterion IDs, but `G5` is already a live criterion in `criteria_routing.json` and `plan_review_G5.md` (decomposition judgment). The plan simultaneously says the new overlays 'match T10/T11' (implying Txx-style IDs) but never names the actual criterion IDs that will be registered. This ambiguity means the is_overlay() regex (`r'T\d+[a-e]?'` at registry.py:383) — which gates overlay-routing behavior in route_criteria (line 364) and sizing.py (line 305) — may not apply to the new criteria if they are given non-Txx IDs.
findings.location: What (REDESIGNED) section — 'Register TWO new LLM-routed overlay criteria (overlay_routing: llm, matching T10/T11)'
findings.suggested_fix: Use Txx-style IDs (e.g. T13 for prohibition-enumeration, T14 for CI-trigger) so `is_overlay()` recognizes them without code change, matching the stated 'prefer reusing T10/T11's existing llm overlay path' intent. Document the chosen IDs in the edit targets.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether the two new overlay criteria will use Txx IDs (the established overlay naming convention that `is_overlay()` and downstream consumers depend on) or whether `is_overlay()`, `sizing.py`, and the prompt-library index need to be widened to recognise non-Txx overlay criteria before committing to G-5/G-10 IDs.
findings.finding: The plan asserts it will 'reuse T10/T11's existing llm overlay path' for the two new overlay criteria, but `is_overlay()` in registry.py is hardcoded to `re.fullmatch(r'T\d+[a-e]?', crit_id)` — only Txx IDs match. Non-Txx overlay criteria (G-5/G-10 styled IDs) will return `is_overlay=False` from `prompt_library._is_overlay_criterion()`, be miscounted in `sizing.py`'s overlay_agent/core_agent split, and silently bypass the overlay-identity checks assumed by the test suite (`test_prompt_library_writes.py`). Whether this matters enough to require Txx IDs or an `is_overlay()` extension is unresolved.
findings.location: What (edit targets) / What (REDESIGNED) — 'prefer reusing T10/T11's existing llm overlay path'
findings.suggested_fix: Either assign the new criteria Txx IDs (e.g. T13/T14) — which automatically satisfies `is_overlay()`, sizing, and the prompt-library index with zero code changes — or explicitly add `G5_prohibition` / `G10_citrigger` as recognised overlay IDs in `is_overlay()` and update sizing + prompt_library accordingly, with a test asserting the new IDs return `is_overlay=True`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Complete the enumeration of `criteria_routing.json` consumers outside `plan_review/` (at minimum: `sizing.py`, `attest.py`, `prompt_library.py`, `criteria/overlay.py`, CI workflow gates) and confirm each requires no change or add them to edit targets.
findings.finding: The plan states 'Enumerate consumers of criteria_routing.json schema before adding' as a work step but never actually enumerates them, leaving unenumerated consumers outside `plan_review/`. Confirmed consumers in other directories include: `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` (shared overlay core), `src/rebar/llm/prompting/prompt_library.py` (is_overlay_criterion), `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` (CANONICAL_LLM sorted list in attestation), `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sizing.py` (is_overlay splits), `.github/workflows/test.yml:239` and `gerrit-verify.yaml:188` (CI parity gate). None of these are discussed in the plan.
findings.location: What (edit targets) — 'Enumerate consumers of criteria_routing.json schema before adding'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete criterion IDs (not gap-report labels) for the two new overlay criteria, explicitly confirming they do not collide with existing CANONICAL_LLM entries (G5 is taken; G10 is available but non-Txx).
findings.finding: The gap-report label `G-5` (prohibition-enumeration overlay) collides with the existing canonical criterion `G5` (decomposition judgment, already in CANONICAL_LLM with a populated prompt file `plan_review_G5.md` and routing entry). The plan never acknowledges this naming collision or specifies what actual criterion IDs it will register.
findings.location: What (REDESIGNED) — 'G-5 prohibition-enumeration overlay'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What (edit targets)' section, replace the gap-report labels G-5 and G-10 with the intended registered criterion IDs (e.g. T13 and T14, or project.prohibition_enum and project.ci_trigger if project-scoped), and add a note cross-referencing that G5 (decomposition) is an unrelated existing criterion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all consumers of `criteria_routing.json` (at minimum: `route_criteria` in orchestrator.py, the registry validator, and any schema-validation hooks) and add an AC confirming each tolerates the new G-5/G-10 entries — or confirm the existing `validate-routing` AC already exercises all consumer paths.
findings.finding: The plan directs 'Enumerate consumers of criteria_routing.json schema before adding' but never actually enumerates them, leaving the CONSUME leg of the shared-state lifecycle unverified. No AC checks that existing consumers (e.g. route_criteria, registry validator, any downstream tooling) tolerate the two new entries without breakage.
findings.location: ## What (edit targets) — 'Enumerate consumers of criteria_routing.json schema before adding'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line to the AC section: 'All known consumers of criteria_routing.json (list them: route_criteria L332, registry validator, …) pass with the two new entries; verified by running the full overlay integration test suite, not only validate-routing.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the output schema for the G-10 abstain path: specify whether ABSTAIN is a top-level criterion status (no findings emitted, coverage record notes 'unsupported CI format') or a per-trigger enum value, and add this to the prompt specification.
findings.finding: The G-10 abstain fallback ('Abstain-with-coverage on unparseable CI (polyglot fail-open)') references a structured output path that is never defined. The normal output schema emits per-trigger INCLUDED/EXCLUDED/NO_FILTER classifications; the abstain path must emit a *different* structured artifact. The plan does not specify whether ABSTAIN is a fourth enum value on per-trigger entries, a top-level criterion-level status field, or a separate output schema branch — leaving the fallback output contract undefined.
findings.location: What (REDESIGNED) section — G-10 bullet; AC4 ('fail-open abstain case on unknown CI')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or fold into AC #1) that verifies the consumer enumeration was performed — e.g. a checklist item in the PR, a comment in criteria_routing.json, or a CI lint step that lists all consumers of the schema.
findings.finding: The plan body contains a described work step — 'Enumerate consumers of criteria_routing.json schema before adding' — that has no corresponding acceptance criterion verifying it was done. If this enumeration step is required (the plan treats it as mandatory pre-work), an implementer could skip it with no AC failure.
findings.location: What (edit targets) section — 'Enumerate consumers of criteria_routing.json schema before adding'; Acceptance Criteria section (no matching AC)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC (or extend AC3/AC4) that exercises the new G-5 and G-10 overlay criteria through the live `route_criteria` path end-to-end (e.g., an integration test using the real LLM client against the registered criteria), or explicitly document a rationale for deferring live-path validation to a named follow-on story.
findings.finding: The G-5 and G-10 acceptance criteria prove correctness only via `pytest -k g5_prohibition` and `pytest -k g10_citrigger` in `tests/unit/` — unit tests that are likely to use mocked or fixture LLM responses — and provide no end-to-end check that the live LLM actually routes and classifies correctly through the new overlay path, violating E6(f) for a new external-LLM-routed code path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3/AC4; tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the ACs whether the g5_prohibition and g10_citrigger pytest cases invoke real LLM calls (or use recorded/VCR fixtures faithful to production); if they use canned mocks that bypass the LLM, add at least one live/end-to-end acceptance criterion (e.g. a smoke-test against a real or recorded LLM call) that validates the routing decision itself.
findings.finding: The acceptance-criteria tests for G-5 and G-10 recall/false-positive cases are located in `tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py`, but the plan does not specify whether these invoke real LLM calls or use canned/mocked responses. Since LLM routing IS the new behavior being introduced (the whole motivation for replacing lexical triggers), tests that mock or stub the LLM would bypass the routing decision entirely — the suite goes green without ever validating that the LLM correctly fires on a prohibition plan and abstains on a descriptive plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `pytest -k g5_prohibition` and `pytest -k g10_citrigger` in `tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py`
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'At least one recall and one false-positive case for each overlay runs against a real LLM call (or a VCR-recorded cassette captured from a real call) so the routing prompt is exercised end-to-end. Mark synthetic-mock-only runs as unit-coverage, not routing-validation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC #5 to match the format of ACs #3–4: specify the test function name, file path (e.g. tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py), the pytest -k selector, and whether it covers G-5, G-10, or both.
findings.finding: The false-positive AC (#5) lacks a verification command and test-file reference, unlike every other AC. It states 'Proof: false-fire case in the eval suite' without specifying the test name, file, or pytest invocation, making it unverifiable by the same standard as the other ACs.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — last bullet: 'False-positive case: a plan that merely DESCRIBES enforcement (no new prohibition) does not fire. Proof: false-fire case in the eval suite.'
findings.suggested_fix: 'False-positive case: a plan that merely DESCRIBES enforcement (no new prohibition) does not fire G-5 or G-10. Proof: author `test_g5_false_positive` (and optionally `test_g10_false_positive`) in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py; `pytest -k "g5_false_positive or g10_false_positive"` green.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-line decision rule for each enum label directly in the plan body (e.g. 'MIGRATED = call site updated to comply; EXEMPTED = explicitly carved out by the prohibition rule; UNCOVERED = call site violates the new prohibition with no carveout').
findings.finding: The per-label classification decision rules for MIGRATED/EXEMPTED/UNCOVERED (G-5) and INCLUDED/EXCLUDED/NO_FILTER (G-10) are referenced as 'define this enum in the prompt' but no decision protocol is co-located in the plan. The plan names the vocabulary but leaves the discriminating logic — what distinguishes EXEMPTED from UNCOVERED, or EXCLUDED from NO_FILTER — entirely unspecified for the executor.
findings.location: What (REDESIGNED) section — G-5 and G-10 bullet descriptions

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a fail-open / abstain-with-coverage behavior for G-5 analogous to G-10's 'polyglot fail-open', and add a corresponding error-path test case in the G-5 eval suite.
findings.finding: G-5 (prohibition-enumeration overlay) has no documented fail-open / graceful-degradation path when the LLM call fails or returns unparseable output, in contrast to G-10 which explicitly specifies 'Abstain-with-coverage on unparseable CI (polyglot fail-open)'.
findings.location: ## What (REDESIGNED) — G-5 bullet
findings.suggested_fix: In the G-5 prompt and routing config, add: on LLM parse failure, emit a single finding of class ABSTAIN with a coverage note (e.g., 'G-5 could not parse call sites; prohibition coverage unverified') rather than raising. Mirror G-10's AC with a `pytest -k g5_failopen` case.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the open-ended 'Enumerate consumers' bullet with a concrete decision: either (a) list known consumers here and state they are forward-compatible with adding new overlay_routing: llm entries, or (b) add an explicit AC that `validate-routing` and any known consumer integrations remain passing after the schema addition.
findings.finding: The 'What (edit targets)' section contains the bullet 'Enumerate consumers of criteria_routing.json schema before adding' — this is an open investigation task deferred to the executor with no stated outcome, decision criteria, or fallback, making it a scope-boundary placeholder.
findings.location: What (edit targets) — 'Enumerate consumers of criteria_routing.json schema before adding'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Verified consumers: [list them]. All consumers treat unknown overlay_routing values as pass-through / are forward-compatible — no schema migration needed.' Or add an AC: 'Existing consumers of criteria_routing.json (list) pass their own validation after the two new entries are added.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a CLAUDE.md / ADR entry capturing the LLM-routed overlay criterion pattern: why LLM routing over lexical, the per-criterion-call model, and the prompt-enum convention — so future overlay authors have a durable reference.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern (LLM-routed overlay criteria with prompt-defined classification enums), but no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc artifact is planned to capture the decision rationale (why LLM routing over lexical triggers, the overlay-per-criterion-call model, enum conventions).
findings.location: ## What (REDESIGNED per review) / ## What (edit targets)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section to CLAUDE.md (or docs/adr/overlay-criteria-pattern.md) recording: (1) decision to use LLM routing over lexical triggers and rationale, (2) the overlay-per-criterion-call model, (3) the prompt-defined enum convention (MIGRATED/EXEMPTED/UNCOVERED etc.), (4) the fail-open/abstain contract.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add structured log emission (at minimum: criterion id, trigger decision, LLM call outcome, abstain reason if any) for both G-5 and G-10 overlay calls, and reference this in at least one AC or edit target.
findings.finding: The plan adds two new LLM call paths (G-5 and G-10 overlay criteria), but no observability instrumentation (log lines, metrics, traces, or alerts) is specified for these new failure points.
findings.location: ## What (edit targets) / ## Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: In orchestrator.py's route_criteria handler for llm overlays, emit a structured log entry: {criterion: 'G-5'|'G-10', triggered: bool, outcome: 'findings'|'abstain'|'error', reason: str}. Add an AC: 'On abstain, a structured log line with criterion id and reason is emitted.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing` is a pre-existing command (cite the module/function) OR add a step to the edit-targets list that creates/extends it as part of this story.
findings.finding: AC1's proving command `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing` is not created by any described step in the plan; if this command does not already exist in the codebase, AC1 is unprovable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing exits 0'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 'What (edit targets)': 'rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py — if validate-routing subcommand does not exist, add a validate_routing() function that loads criteria_routing.json and asserts all entries with overlay_routing: llm have a corresponding prompt file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign concrete Txx IDs (e.g. T13/T14) to the two new overlay criteria so `is_overlay()` (registry.py:383, `r'T\d+[a-e]?'`) recognizes them as overlays; confirm the gap-report labels G-5/G-10 are provenance tags only, not production criterion IDs.
findings.finding: The plan uses gap-report provenance labels 'G-5' and 'G-10' as the proposed production criterion IDs, but `is_overlay()` (registry.py:383) matches only `r'T\d+[a-e]?'`. Criteria named G-5 or G-10 would NOT be recognized as overlays, causing orchestrator.py:364 to skip the overlay-routing guard (they'd always enter the finder regardless of trigger state) and sizing.py:305–306 to bucket them as core-agent instead of overlay-agent — both contradicting the plan's stated 'own context window / LLM-routed overlay' architecture.
findings.location: What (REDESIGNED) section — 'Register TWO new LLM-routed overlay criteria (overlay_routing: llm, matching T10/T11)'; also AC line 1 proof command
findings.suggested_fix: Name the new criteria T13 (prohibition-enumeration) and T14 (CI-trigger/release-infra) in criteria_routing.json and CANONICAL_LLM. The plan's 'Provenance' section already explains that G-*/T-* gap tags are NOT codebase symbols — apply the same logic to the production IDs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the criterion ID namespace: either assign T-series IDs (e.g. T13/T14) so `is_overlay()` and the overlay suppression path at orchestrator.py:364 work correctly, OR extend `is_overlay()` to also match the chosen ID pattern AND verify the overlay suppression path fires as intended.
findings.finding: The proposed new overlay criteria use G-prefixed IDs (gap-report labels 'G-5', 'G-10'), but `is_overlay()` in registry.py only matches the pattern `T\d+[a-e]?`. A G-prefixed criterion ID returns `is_overlay()==False`, so the LLM-routing gate at orchestrator.py:364 (`if registry.is_overlay(cid) and cid in triggers and not triggers[cid]: continue`) never applies — the criteria would always enter the finder unconditionally, not LLM-routed as overlays. The plan claims to 'reuse T10/T11's existing llm overlay path', but that path works because T10/T11 match the T-prefix. G-prefixed IDs bypass overlay suppression entirely.
findings.location: What (REDESIGNED) section — 'overlay_routing: llm, matching T10/T11'; What (edit targets) — criteria_routing.json entry
findings.suggested_fix: Assign T-series IDs (e.g. T13 for prohibition-enumeration, T14 for CI-trigger) — these are overlays by design, match the existing T\d+ is_overlay() regex, reuse T10/T11's llm overlay_routing path without any registry.py regex change, and slot naturally into AGENT_TIER alongside T10/T11.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the actual criterion IDs to be registered: either use T-prefixed IDs (e.g. T13/T14) so `is_overlay()` and the overlay suppression path work as described, or document that the G-prefix IDs will NOT use the overlay suppression path and will instead rely solely on the LLM's PASS-not-applicable response.
findings.finding: The plan's new criterion IDs ('G-5 prohibition-enumeration overlay', 'G-10 CI-trigger/release-infra overlay') are non-T-prefixed, but `is_overlay()` uses `re.fullmatch(r'T\d+[a-e]?', crit_id)` (registry.py:383-384). A G-5 or G-10 ID will never be classified as an overlay, so `route_criteria`'s overlay suppression logic (orchestrator.py:364: `if registry.is_overlay(cid) and cid in triggers and not triggers[cid]: continue`) will never fire — the new criteria will always be included regardless of LLM routing intent, defeating the overlay pattern.
findings.location: What (REDESIGNED) section — 'G-5 prohibition-enumeration overlay' and 'G-10 CI-trigger/release-infra overlay'; registry.py:383-384

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the production criterion IDs explicitly (must match `T\d+[a-e]?` to be treated as overlays by `is_overlay()`) and add `registry.py` to the edit targets to update both `CANONICAL_LLM` and `AGENT_TIER` frozensets.
findings.finding: The plan uses 'G-5' and 'G-10' as criterion IDs (gap-report labels), but `is_overlay()` at registry.py:383 matches only `r'T\d+[a-e]?'`. A G-prefixed ID will NOT be recognized as an overlay by `route_criteria()` (orchestrator.py:364), which uses `is_overlay(cid)` to decide whether to skip deterministically-untriggered overlays. The two new overlay criteria will be misrouted: they will fire on EVERY plan (not LLM-gated) as non-overlay 1-TURN/AGENT criteria, or silently mis-behave, depending on ID format chosen.
findings.location: What (REDESIGNED) section / What (edit targets) section; registry.py:383; orchestrator.py:364
findings.suggested_fix: Assign T-namespace IDs (e.g. T13 for prohibition-enumeration, T14 for CI-trigger). Add `registry.py` to edit targets: add the new IDs to `CANONICAL_LLM` frozenset (line 84) and, if exec:AGENT, to `AGENT_TIER` (line 77).

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
